name: Finger Pass
advice: Keep wrist straight and fingers together.|Hit the ball with fingertips for better control.|Follow through the shot to give the ball correct direction.|Practice placing the ball exactly where you want it.
next_steps: Practice finger passes against wall 50 times per day.|Film yourself from front and back and compare.|Practice with partner: exchange finger passes 10 in a row without drop.|Focus on wrist movement next session.

name: Bump
advice: Use whole arm to create power.|Keep wrist relaxed for better feel.|Hit the ball in front of body.|Focus on getting the ball over net with precision.
next_steps: Practice bump against wall with steady tempo.|Train footwork – position yourself right before shot.|Practice with partner: serve–bump 20 in a row.|Work on arm angle at contact.

name: Underhand Serve
advice: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.|Hit the ball with forearm, not hand.|Keep ball still before serve.|Practice varying serve direction and speed.
next_steps: Practice serve precision: aim at specific zones.|Vary power – train both low and high speed.|Film serve from side to check body position.|Practice 20 serves in a row with consistent technique.

name: Overhand Serve
advice: Toss the ball up and hit it as it falls.|Use whole body to generate power.|Keep eyes on ball the whole time.|Practice hitting ball in center for better control.
next_steps: Practice toss separately – consistent height and position.|Focus on hip rotation for more power.|Train varying direction (cross/line).|Film from side – check arm angle height.

name: Spike
advice: Jump high and stretch arm completely.|Contact ball in front of forehead.|Use wrist to direct the ball.|Follow through shot for maximum power.
next_steps: Practice approach – 3-step approach until it sits right.|Train jump separately to build explosivity.|Practice spike with net without opposition.|Film from front – check contact height.

name: Alternative Attacks
advice: Vary your attacks to surprise opponent.|Practice different types of shots like tip and roll.|Use body to create angles.|Communicate with partner about which attack you'll make.
next_steps: Practice tip and roll 10 times each per training.|Train late decision-making in jump.|Practice with partner: blocker chooses side, attacker adapts.|Film side profile – check how well you hide attack intention.

name: Block
advice: Jump at same time as opponent.|Keep arms straight and hands together.|Read opponent's intention.|Block ball straight up to help your partner.
next_steps: Practice block timing with partner who spikes.|Train reading opponent's shoulder direction.|Practice stable landing and immediate next position.|Film from front – check hand position at contact.

name: Backward Block
advice: Rotate body to reach ball behind you.|Use both hands for better control.|Jump high and stretch out.|Practice timing to arrive in time.
next_steps: Practice rotation and stretching without ball.|Train backward block with slow serve from partner.|Work on landing – direct balance after jump.|Film from behind – check rotation's full reach.

name: Serve Reception
advice: Stand ready with knees bent.|Move feet quickly to reach ball.|Use flat hand for better control.|Pass ball high and precise to partner.
next_steps: Practice reception precision – aim at specific zone.|Train reaction speed with varying serve directions.|Practice "bump-position" as base position.|Film from front during match to analyze decisions.

name: Jump Float Serve
advice: Jump high and hit ball with fingertips.|Keep wrist straight.|Create a floating motion.|Practice placing ball exactly.
next_steps: Practice float motion standing on ground first.|Train approach + jump without ball.|Practice precision: aim at specific fields.|Film serve from side to check jump height.

name: Spin Serve
advice: Spin ball with fingers before serve.|Hit ball with power to create rotation.|Vary spin to make it harder for opponent.|Practice hitting ball right for desired effect.
next_steps: Practice grip – hold and spin ball without serving.|Train spin direction: topspin vs. sidespin.|Practice 10 spins in a row to target field.|Film hand at contact – check finger work.

next_steps: Practice finger passes against wall 50 times per day.|Film yourself from front – check hand position.|Practice with partner in pair-finger passes 10 in a row.|Focus on setting height and precision.

next_steps: Practice bump against wall with steady tempo.|Train footwork – position yourself right.|Practice serve–reception with partner 20 in a row.|Work on arm angle at contact.

next_steps: Practice 3-step approach until it sits right.|Train jump for explosivity.|Practice spike with net without opposition.|Film from front – check contact height.

name: Serve Reception

next_steps: Practice reception–pass–setting in group of 3.|Train reaction speed with varying serves.|Practice precision: aim at setter position.|Film match to analyze position decisions.

name: Jump Float Serve
advice: Jump high and hit ball with fingertips.|Keep wrist straight.|Create a floating motion.|Practice placing ball exactly.
next_steps: Practice float motion standing on ground.|Train approach + jump without ball.|Practice precision to specific fields.|Film from side – check jump height.

name: Spin Serve
advice: Spin ball with fingers before serve.|Hit ball with power to create rotation.|Vary spin to make it harder for opponent.|Practice hitting ball right for desired effect.
next_steps: Practice grip and ball rotation separately.|Train topspin vs. sidespin.|Practice 10 spins in a row to target field.|Film hand at contact – check finger work.

name: Forehand
advice: Prepare early with a clear split step before your opponent makes contact.|Meet the ball in front of your body for better control and pace.|Rotate hips and shoulders together through the shot for stable power.|Finish high and balanced for a more consistent ball trajectory.
next_steps: Do 3 x 20 crosscourt forehands focusing on early preparation.|Record from the side and check that your contact point is in front of the hip.|Alternate 10 crosscourt shots and 10 down-the-line shots.|Finish the session with 15 forehands in a row at controlled tempo.

name: Backhand
advice: Load early with the racket back and a stable shoulder line.|Keep your head still all the way through contact.|Use legs and core to generate power instead of relying only on the arm.|Drive the follow-through clearly toward your intended target.
next_steps: Hit 30 backhands against a wall with steady tempo and net clearance.|Practice backhand on the move with 5 sets of lateral footwork drills.|Record from behind to review shoulder rotation and balance.|Complete 12 backhands in a row with focus on depth.

name: Serve
advice: Keep your toss consistent in both height and placement on every serve.|Create a clear knee bend and drive upward through the legs.|Contact the ball at the highest reachable point with a relaxed arm.|Land in control and be ready for the next shot immediately.
next_steps: Practice 20 tosses without hitting until they land in the same window.|Serve 3 x 10 first serves to a chosen target area.|Alternate precision sets and pace sets.|Record from the side to analyze contact point and timing.

name: Net Volley
advice: A short backswing and a firm racket face create a stable volley.|Use small adjustment steps so contact happens in front of the body.|Keep your center of gravity forward with slightly bent knees.|Place volleys into open space instead of forcing maximum power.
next_steps: Do 4 x 12 forehand volleys close to the net with a control-first focus.|Do 4 x 12 backhand volleys aiming for the same contact point each rep.|Practice baseline-to-net transition with a split step before the volley.|Record from the front and review racket-face angle at contact.
